name: Libya (State of Libya / دولة ليبيا)
summary: Libya is a North African mixed-legal-system state combining French/Italian civil-law substantive heritage (via Italian colonial and Egyptian civil-code transplant inheritances) with Maliki-school Islamic-law personal-status jurisdiction. Family-law framework operates under the Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law 10/1984 (substantially amended 2015), governing custody (hadana) and guardianship (wilaya) at arts. 62-72. The Supreme Court (المحكمة العليا) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court operates constitutional review. Personal-status matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Family Court). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Libya is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child standard. Libya is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.citation: Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law 10/1984 arts. 62-72
statutory_framework.title: Marriage and Divorce Law — Custody and guardianship
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal personal-status statute drawing primarily on Maliki Islamic-law jurisprudence. Substantially amended 2015. Arts. 62-72 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship).

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code drawing on French civil-law via Egyptian Civil Code transplant + Italian civil-law colonial inheritance.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (المحكمة العليا)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Libya
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Libyan family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Italian colonial framework + Italo-Turkish War + Libya-Italia 1911-1943
key_developments.description: Italy invaded Libya 29 September 1911 in Italo-Turkish War. Italian-Libya (Libia Italiana) 1911-1943 substantively distinctive Maghreb Italian-colonial framework establishing Italian-civil-law inheritance. Foundational pre-independence colonial framework affecting subsequent legal-system framework.

key_developments.title: Libya independence + King Idris I + Federal-Kingdom framework
key_developments.description: Libya independence 24 December 1951 from UN administration following Italian-territorial-loss WWII — establishing federal Kingdom of Libya under King Idris I. Substantively distinctive Maghreb federal-monarchy framework. Foundational pre-Gaddafi framework affecting subsequent constitutional-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Civil Code 1953
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code enacted drawing on French civil-law via Egyptian Civil Code transplant (Sanhuri framework) + Italian civil-law colonial inheritance. Foundational substantive-civil-law framework for contemporary family-law-jurisprudence persisting through subsequent regime-changes.

key_developments.title: Gaddafi coup d'état + Libyan Arab Jamahiriya + 42-year-revolutionary framework
key_developments.description: Muammar Gaddafi coup d'état 1 September 1969 ending Idris I monarchy. Subsequent Libyan Arab Jamahiriya 1977-2011 — substantively distinctive Maghreb revolutionary-state framework. Foundational 42-year Gaddafi-era framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law 10/1984
key_developments.description: Federal personal-status statute enacted codifying personal-status matters on Maliki jurisprudential basis. Arts. 62-72 govern hadana (custody) and wilaya (guardianship). Foundational Islamic-law personal-status framework.

key_developments.title: Libya ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Libya ratified the UNCRC on 15 April 1993 (with reservations consistent with Islamic Sharia) — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration applied through Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law + Maliki Islamic-law framework.

key_developments.title: Libyan Revolution + Gaddafi-fall + NATO-intervention + Civil-War framework
key_developments.description: Libyan Revolution 17 February 2011 substantively ending 42-year Gaddafi era. NATO-intervention 19 March-31 October 2011 (Operation Unified Protector). Gaddafi death 20 October 2011. Substantive 21st-century Arab-Spring framework substantially disrupting political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Libyan Civil War 2014-2020 + dual-government framework + Skhirat Agreement 2015
key_developments.description: Libyan Civil War 2014-2020 substantively distinctive Maghreb post-revolution dual-government framework — Government of National Accord (Tripoli) vs Libyan National Army (Tobruk). Skhirat Agreement 17 December 2015 substantially affecting political-institutional framework. Substantive political-institutional disruption affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law amendments + civil-war-era statutory framework
key_developments.description: Substantial amendments to personal-status provisions during civil-war-era — substantively affecting custody (hadana) and guardianship (wilaya) framework. Substantive statutory-reform within turbulent political-institutional context.

key_developments.title: Derna flood disaster + dual-government framework + LPA framework continuation
key_developments.description: Derna flood disaster 10-11 September 2023 (Storm Daniel) — substantively distinctive Maghreb climate-disaster framework affecting 11,000+ deaths and 10,000+ missing. Dual-government framework continuing under Tripoli-based Government of National Unity (GNU) and Tobruk-based House of Representatives + LNA framework. Libyan Political Agreement (LPA) framework continuation affecting institutional-context for family-law adjudication. Substantively significant Maghreb institutional context.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court + Constitutional Court — substantive register + post-civil-war framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Libya and Constitutional Court continue to develop family-law jurisprudence under Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law 10/1984 (as amended 2015) + Civil Code 1953 + Maliki Islamic-law framework in custody disputes within post-civil-war-framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Libya operates a mixed-legal-system framework — French/Italian civil-law substantive (via Egyptian transplant + Italian colonial heritage) + Maliki Islamic-law personal-status. Within the Maghreb Maliki cluster alongside Morocco, Tunisia, Algeria.
Italian civil-law colonial inheritance is structurally distinctive within the Maghreb cluster — Libya's only African corpus jurisdiction with substantial Italian-tradition layering.
Non-Hague Convention status places Libya in the non-Hague Maghreb cluster alongside Algeria — distinct from Hague-acceding Morocco and Tunisia.
editorial_notes: Libya jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Italian-colonial-to-contemporary trajectory: 1911-Italian-colonial-framework + 1951-Libya-independence-+-King-Idris-I-+-Federal-Kingdom + 1953-Civil-Code + 1969-Gaddafi-coup-+-Libyan-Arab-Jamahiriya + 1984-Marriage-Divorce-Related-Matters-Law + 1993-UNCRC-ratification + 2011-Libyan-Revolution-+-Gaddafi-fall-+-NATO-intervention + 2014-Libyan-Civil-War-+-Skhirat-Agreement-2015 + 2015-Marriage-Divorce-Related-Matters-Law-amendments + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Constitutional-Court-post-civil-war.

Mixed-legal-system Maghreb (French/Italian civil-law substantive via Egyptian transplant + Italian colonial heritage + Maliki Islamic-law personal-status). Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law 10/1984 + 2015 amendments + Civil Code 1953 + non-Hague Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under Marriage, Divorce and Related Matters Law 10/1984 + Civil Code 1953 + Maliki Islamic-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins North-African/Maghreb + Maliki + Italian-colonial-heritage-1911-1943-distinctive + Libyan-Arab-Jamahiriya-1977-2011-Gaddafi-42-year + Libyan-Revolution-2011-+-NATO-intervention + Libyan-Civil-War-2014-2020-+-dual-government-Tripoli-Tobruk-+-Skhirat-2015 + Sanhuri-Egyptian-Civil-Code-derivative + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
